examples.document_content: Enable Modbus Communication Using Embedded Webpage. Enable/disable Modbus TCP communication using the embedded webpage. Pre-Conditions: Panel Server powered ON, Network connected, Webpage accessible. Steps & Expected Results: Open Communication Settings â†’ Page displayed, Enable Modbus TCP â†’ Enabled successfully, Disable Modbus TCP â†’ Disabled successfully. Final Result: User can enable/disable Modbus TCP without errors.

test_cases.title: Enable Modbus Communication Using Embedded Webpage
test_cases.description: Enable/disable Modbus TCP communication using the embedded webpage.
test_cases.objective: Verify that Modbus TCP can be enabled and disabled through the embedded web interface
acceptance_criteria: User can enable Modbus TCP from webpage
User can disable Modbus TCP from webpage
No error messages during enable/disable operations
test_cases.final_result: User can enable/disable Modbus TCP without errors

examples.document_content: Configure Modbus Serial Line (RS-485) Communication Parameters. Configure baud rate, parity, and stop bits. Pre-Conditions: Panel Server ON and connected, Admin access available, RS-485 device connected. Steps: Login to webpage, Navigate to Modbus settings, Set parameters (19200, Even, 1), Save configuration, Verify device discovery. Final Result: RS-485 communication works correctly.

test_cases.title: Configure Modbus Serial Line (RS-485) Communication Parameters
test_cases.description: Configure baud rate, parity, and stop bits.
test_cases.objective: Verify RS-485 communication parameters can be configured correctly

examples.document_content: Wireless Device Discovery and Pairing. Discover and pair wireless PowerTag sensors. Steps: Login to webpage, Start device discovery, Select detected device, Pair device, Assign label, Verify real-time data. Final Result: Device paired and data visible.

test_cases.title: Wireless Device Discovery and Pairing
test_cases.description: Discover and pair wireless PowerTag sensors.
test_cases.objective: Verify wireless devices can be discovered and successfully paired with real-time data visibility

examples.document_content: Configure Ethernet Network Settings (Switched Mode). Configure ETH1 & ETH2 in switched mode. Steps: Login, Open Network settings, Select Switched Mode, Assign static IP, Save settings, Verify access. Final Result: Device accessible using new IP.

test_cases.title: Configure Ethernet Network Settings (Switched Mode)
test_cases.description: Configure ETH1 & ETH2 in switched mode.
test_cases.objective: Verify Ethernet ports can be configured in switched mode with static IP
preconditions: Ethernet cables connected to ETH1 and ETH2

examples.document_content: Assign Virtual Modbus Server ID. Assign unique virtual ID to downstream device. Steps: Discover device, Assign virtual ID (e.g., 10), Save configuration, Test from Modbus client, Check duplicate ID handling. Final Result: Device accessible via assigned ID.

test_cases.title: Assign Virtual Modbus Server ID
test_cases.description: Assign unique virtual ID to downstream device.
test_cases.objective: Verify virtual Modbus server IDs can be assigned to downstream devices
expected_results: Device responds to Modbus client via assigned ID

examples.document_content: User Account Management. Create users and test account lockout. Steps: Login as admin, Create user, Attempt wrong login multiple times, Verify lockout, Unlock user, Login successfully. Final Result: User management and lockout works.

test_cases.title: User Account Management
test_cases.description: Create users and test account lockout.
test_cases.objective: Verify user account creation and lockout mechanisms
acceptance_criteria: Account lockout triggers after failed attempts

examples.document_content: Firmware Update via EPC Software. Update firmware using EPC software. Steps: Open EPC software, Detect Panel Server, Select firmware file, Start update, Wait for reboot, Verify new version. Final Result: Firmware updated successfully.

test_cases.title: Firmware Update via EPC Software
test_cases.objective: Verify firmware can be updated through EPC software

examples.document_content: Configure Data Publication to Cloud. Send energy data to EcoStruxure Energy Hub. Steps: Open Data Publication settings, Select cloud service, Enter API details, Set interval, Save settings, Verify cloud data. Final Result: Data visible in cloud dashboard.

test_cases.title: Configure Data Publication to Cloud
test_cases.description: Send energy data to EcoStruxure Energy Hub.
test_cases.objective: Verify energy data can be published to the cloud dashboard
preconditions: Energy meters connected and reporting data

examples.document_content: Configure Overload Alarm. Enable overload alarm on energy meter. Steps: Open meter settings, Enable alarm, Set threshold (5000 W), Apply load > threshold, Verify alarm, Reduce load. Final Result: Alarm triggers and clears correctly.

test_cases.title: Configure Overload Alarm
test_cases.description: Enable overload alarm on energy meter.
test_cases.objective: Verify overload alarms can be configured and trigger correctly
acceptance_criteria: Alarm clears when load drops below threshold

examples.document_content: Configure Modbus TCP/IP Filtering. Restrict Modbus access to authorized IPs. Steps: Enable IP filtering, Add allowed IP, Save settings, Test authorized access, Test unauthorized access, Disable filtering. Final Result: Authorized access allowed, others blocked.

test_cases.title: Configure Modbus TCP/IP Filtering
test_cases.description: Restrict Modbus access to authorized IPs.
test_cases.objective: Verify Modbus TCP/IP filtering restricts access to authorized IPs only
expected_results: Filtering disabled, all IPs can access

examples.document_content:
EcoStruxure Panel Server Webpages Overview
The web server embedded in the EcoStruxure Panel Server provides webpages
enabling you to commission electrical products and systems compatible with the
EcoStruxure Panel Server in your switchboard.
In addition the webpages provide real-time data and active alarm visualization,
and historical analysis from devices (depending on the model), for entry-level
energy and operation management.
Electrical product and commissioning capabilities allow you to:
• Discover wireless devices installed in the EcoStruxure Panel Server system
(depending on the model).
• Discover Modbus-SL and Modbus TCP/IP devices from EcoStruxure Panel
Server (depending on the model).
• Configure I/O devices connected to the EcoStruxure Panel Server through
the digital inputs (depending on the model).
Energy and operation monitoring capabilities allow you to:
• Visualize real-time data and events through a supported web browser.
• Monitor and control connected devices.
• Publish data to an SFTP or HTTPS server or to the Schneider Electric cloud.
• Visualize trending on historical data, up to a period of 3 years (Advanced
model).
• Export selected logged data to your PC for additional analysis (Advanced
model).
For more detailed information about the webpages, refer to Using EcoStruxure
Panel Server Webpages, page 190.
DOCA0172EN-19 33
EcoStruxure Panel Server Webpages Overview EcoStruxure Panel Server
EcoStruxure Power Commission (EPC) Software
Overview
EcoStruxure Power Commission is intelligent software that helps you set up, test,
and commission the electrical products and systems in your switchboards with
efficiency and ease. It also allows you to generate a dedicated QR code for the
electrical panel to enable digital collaboration with contractors and facility
managers by sharing important documentation.
Key Features
EcoStruxure Power Commission software performs the following actions for the
supported devices :
• Easy setup
◦ Discover all the smart d

test_cases.title: Giving access to cloud connected gateways and devices to associate
test_cases.description: Giving access to cloud connected gateways and devices to associate required models. A custom device model in the EcoStruxure Power Commission Web tool contains the list of measurements, alarms, Modbus frames and events that can be used to monitor Modbus based devices in the field. The user can create, edit, view, and export device model data as per the requirement.
